user_query:
Please read input/case_packet.json and prepare a legal-precedent analysis brief for the hypothetical contract dispute described there. Create two files:

1) Write output/precedent_brief.md structured with the following top-level sections and headings, in this order:
- Case Name & Citation
- Facts
- Issue
- Holding
- Reasoning (Ratio Decidendi)
- Dicta
- Court Hierarchy
- Binding Authorities
- Persuasive Authorities
- Distinguishing Plan
- Overruling & Modifying Options
- Research & Validation
- IRAC Example
- Checklist

Content requirements for output/precedent_brief.md:
- Under Court Hierarchy (or within your hierarchy discussion), include the exact circuit mapping line: 7th:   IL, IN, WI
- In Research & Validation, explicitly include the words "Shepardize" and "KeyCite" (you can state what you would do, not actually run any tools)
- Under Binding Authorities and Persuasive Authorities, list the authorities from input/case_packet.json according to each item's authority_hint (place all items marked binding in the Binding section and all marked persuasive in the Persuasive section). List one authority per line starting with "- ".
- Include an IRAC Example containing clearly labeled lines for: Issue:, Rule:, Application:, Conclusion:
- In the Checklist section, include a checkbox list. Include the following exact items (verbatim) among your checklist entries:
  - [ ] Full case name and citation recorded
  - [ ] Material facts identified and listed
  - [ ] Case Shepardized / KeyCited
  - [ ] Binding or persuasive authority determined
  - [ ] Signal word appropriate (see, cf., but see)
- Do not add or cite any real cases beyond what is provided in the input file. Keep your analysis generic and based on the hypothetical provided.

2) Write output/validation.json with the following fields:
- binding_count: the number of list items you placed under the "Binding Authorities" section in output/precedent_brief.md
- persuasive_count: the number of list items you placed under the "Persuasive Authorities" section
- found_shepardize: true if the word "Shepardize" appears anywhere in output/precedent_brief.md, otherwise false
- found_keycite: true if the word "KeyCite" appears anywhere in output/precedent_brief.md, otherwise false

Assume this case is in the US federal system in Illinois (7th Circuit). Use only the hypothetical material from input/case_packet.json. Write all files to the specified output/ paths.
